task.title: Run Jira Orchestrate for MM-820
task.instructions:
Run Jira Orchestrate for MM-820.

Use the Jira preset brief for MM-820 as the canonical Moon Spec orchestration input.

Source story: STORY-001. Source summary: Complete remaining behavior for Establish Step Execution conformance harness. Source Jira issue: unknown. Original brief reference: not provided. Source design document: docs/Steps/StepExecutionsAndCheckpointing.md.

Classify this input as a single-story feature request. Inspect existing Moon Spec artifacts and resume from the first incomplete stage instead of regenerating valid later-stage artifacts. Use the existing Jira Orchestrate workflow. Do not run implementation inline inside the breakdown task.

inputs.constraints: Source story: STORY-001. Source summary: Complete remaining behavior for Establish Step Execution conformance harness. Source Jira issue: unknown. Original brief reference: not provided. Use the trusted Jira preset brief as canonical input. Treat docs/Steps/StepExecutionsAndCheckpointing.md as runtime source requirements. Inspect existing Moon Spec artifacts and resume from the first incomplete stage. Do not run implementation inline inside the breakdown task.

canonicalJiraBriefSummary.summary: Complete remaining behavior for Establish Step Execution conformance harness
acceptanceCriteria: A single conformance command covers manifest, checkpoint, gate, terminology, and API contract fixtures.
Tests fail when manifests or checkpoints include raw stdout, stderr, diffs, logs, provider payloads, credentials, or oversized inline evidence.
At least one replay or degraded-input fixture exists for old manifest rows, old checkpoint rows, old gate verdict strings, and old ledger rows with only legacy checkpoint refs.
Canonical terms step-executions, executionOrdinal, and recover_from_failed_step are enforced.

Golden fixtures cover successful execution, failed reattempt, gate failure, recovery with preserved steps, degraded checkpoint payload, degraded gate verdict, and legacy ledger rows with only old checkpoint refs.
Canonical content types and terminology are asserted for all new evidence writers.
Compatibility helpers return typed invalid/degraded decisions rather than uncaught exceptions.
alreadyImplementedEvidence: DESIGN-REQ-006 manifest inline evidence rejection in moonmind/schemas/temporal_models.py and tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_step_execution_manifest.py.
DESIGN-REQ-010 checkpoint content type, identity, validation, and degraded payload handling in tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_step_checkpoints.py.
DESIGN-REQ-024 degraded manifest/checkpoint payload coverage in manifest and checkpoint unit tests.
